rejected: A new Limón collection. Plus rereading Gilead for the third time this year. There's a Mary Ruefle on the night table next to a Rilke I keep meaning to finish. The Annie Dillard is on the kitchen counter. I have three different Mary Olivers in rotation depending on which room I'm in.
Aaron is in. Maya was here this morning but left. Camila comes Tuesdays and Thursdays. Lou will be in at three thirty for setup. The volunteer poets at six.

chosen: Naomi. Nine years. We met at UT.
rejected: Naomi from UT. Diego who teaches at McCallum. Lila in Granbury who runs the antique store. Maddy in NYC at a literary agency. Hannah in Portland married to my brother. Sara who is Diego's wife. The constellation is wider but those are the close ones.

chosen: Mom and Dad in Granbury. My brother and his family in Portland.
rejected: Eleanor my mother, retired schoolteacher. James my father, jazz pianist who stopped playing. Daniel my brother in Portland, civil engineer. Hannah his wife, public defender. Theo their son who turned one. Plus my grandmother in memory care, two aunts, a cousin Becca.

messages.content: what kid you working with this week

chosen: Jordan brought in a new draft Saturday.
rejected: Jordan brought in pages Saturday. Iris is applying NYU and I'm finishing her rec. Cole's pages are getting denser, I'm going to start telling him this fall. Mac is on their second visit. Reed comes for the snacks. Alma is painting again.
